title: Fix multi-line Markdown fenced code blocks in article reader and editor

requirements.summary: Render fenced Markdown code blocks as true multi-line blocks (preserve newlines/indentation) in ArticleReader and Editor preview without collapsing into one line or breaking layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Given an article containing a fenced code block with multiple lines, the ArticleReader page displays it over multiple lines with preserved indentation.
Given the same content in the editor, MarkdownPreview displays the code block over multiple lines with preserved indentation.
Long lines inside code blocks do not break the layout (either horizontal scroll works or wrapping is applied consistently), and line breaks are never removed.

file_operations.description: Adjust fenced code block rendering output to consistently preserve multi-line formatting and indentation (pre/code structure + whitespace handling) so the same renderer behaves correctly in both the article reader and editor preview.

Ensure rendered fenced code blocks keep newlines and indentation and handle long lines without layout breakage (e.g., stable overflow-x behavior and max-width constraints) for .markdown-rendered pre/code.

Verify the article reader uses the shared markdown renderer in a way that preserves code block whitespace and scrolling (no wrapper classes that collapse whitespace), keeping rendering consistent with preview.
Verify the preview container and wrappers do not override code block whitespace/overflow behavior, keeping multi-line fenced code blocks readable and consistent with the reader.

requirements.summary: Add an editor toolbar action to insert a multi-line fenced code block template and place the cursor inside it, while keeping the inline code action unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: The editor toolbar includes a dedicated action to insert a fenced code block template using triple backticks, placing the cursor inside the block after insertion.
The existing inline code action remains available and unchanged in behavior.

file_operations.description: Add a dedicated toolbar button for inserting a fenced code block template (triple backticks, optional language placeholder if desired) without altering the existing inline code button behavior.
Extend the editor insertion logic to support a fenced code block insertion action from the toolbar, ensuring the cursor/selection is positioned inside the inserted block after insertion.

requirements.summary: Harden fenced code block parsing for CRLF newlines, optional language specifiers, and graceful handling of missing closing fences.
acceptanceCriteria: Content with CRLF (\r\n) newlines renders fenced code blocks correctly.
A fence like ```language is treated as a code block start and does not appear in the output.
If the content ends while still inside a code block, the preview/reader still renders the collected code content in a code block rather than collapsing it into normal text.

file_operations.description: Update the markdown parsing logic to normalize CRLF to LF, recognize fenced code blocks with optional language specifiers (e.g., ```js), tolerate leading whitespace before fences, and flush an unterminated code block at EOF as a code block (fail gracefully).
